name: Kerr v. United States Dist. Court for Northern Dist. of Cal.
question: Can a writ of mandamus be issued when prisoners suing the district court wish to have the petition for discovery vacated?
description: A case in which the Court held that a writ of mandamus was inappropriate where officials associated with California state prisons system sought to prevent the production of certain documents requested by prisoners in a class action lawsuit about the sentencing and punishment of inmates.
